steps.title: Fastest check: compare a table or graph
steps.content: For an equivalent-expression question, enter the original expression and the answer choices in a calculator table or graph. The correct choice must match for several inputs, not just one. The matching choice is B, $46(x+y)$.

steps.title: Algebra check
steps.content: Use the work below to confirm the identity for every allowed input. This matters because one random matching input is only a quick screen, not a proof that two expressions are always equivalent.

steps.title: Look for the common factor
steps.content: Both terms in $46x+46y$ have a factor of $46$. When a number multiplies every term, pull that number outside parentheses.

steps.title: Factor the expression
steps.content:
$$46x+46y=46\cdot x+46\cdot y=46(x+y)$$
The expression inside the parentheses keeps the two unlike terms, $x$ and $y$, separate.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: An incorrect response multiplies $x$ and $y$, which changes the structure. Choice C (‘$92y$’) is $92y$, and choice D (‘$92x$’) is $92x$, so each drops one variable. The correct response preserves both terms as $46(x+y)$, so B is correct.
